model/output:
* **Prediction Task**: Classification.
  * The model is used for discriminative tasks, specifically classifying the severity of diabetic retinopathy (DR) and predicting the progression of DR.

* **Target Variable**:
  * Referable or sight-threatening DR.
  * Progression of DR over 5 years.
  * Prioritization of patients for DR screening.
  * Classification of DR severity.
  * Prediction of DR progression from intermediate AMD to GA within a month.
  * Prediction of recurrence of nAMD within 3 months after 1-month dry-up.
  * Prediction of the first recurrence after three loading doses.
  * Classification of epiretinal membrane (ERM) severity.
  * Detection and classification of macular telangiectasia type II (MacTel Type II) from OCT scans.

evaluation/method:
**Evaluation Strategy:**

* **Internal Validation**:
  * **Cross-Validation**: Five-fold cross-validation was used to evaluate the performance of models trained on synthesis-balanced public datasets for classifying normal, drusen, diabetic macular edema (DME), and choroidal neovascularization (CNV) in optical coherence tomography (OCT) images.
  * **Hold-out Set**: A hold-out set was used to evaluate the performance of models trained with mixed synthetic and authentic OCT scans for classifying normal, age-related macular degeneration (AMD), and DME.

* **External Validation**:
  * **External Test Set**: An external test set was used to evaluate the performance of models trained on synthesis-balanced public datasets for classifying normal, drusen, DME, and CNV in OCT images.
  * **Multinational Datasets**: External validation was conducted on multinational datasets for a retinopathy of prematurity (ROP) screening model, achieving area under the receiver operating characteristic curve (AUROC) values of 80.8% and 87.8% for more-than-mild ROP (mtmROP) and type 1 ROP, respectively, on a dataset from Aravind Eye Hospital with over 69,000 images.
  * **Real-world Retinal Images**: External validation was conducted on real-world retinal images for the RETFound foundation model, demonstrating robust performance.
evaluation/measure:
Performance Metrics:

* **Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) Prediction Model**:
  * AUROC: 0.72–0.86
  * Sensitivity: 90.38% (model rank), 86.54% (DR grades and HbA1C levels), 68.95% (DR grades only), 50% (random visits)

* **Age-Related Macular Degeneration (AMD) Classification**:
  * Accuracy: 96.85%
  * Sensitivity: 93.72%
  * Specificity: 97.89%

* **Retinopathy of Prematurity (ROP) Screening**:
  * AUROC: 83.5% (mtmROP), 82.2% (type 1 ROP) for Stanford dataset
  * AUROC: 80.8% (mtmROP), 87.8% (type 1 ROP) for Aravind dataset

* **Myopic Maculopathy (MM) Classification**:
  * Sensitivity: 96.86% (tessellated fundus), 75.98% (diffuse chorioretinal atrophy), 64.67% (patchy chorioretinal atrophy), 88.75% (macular atrophy)
  * Specificity: >93% for each severity level

* **OCT Image Classification for AMD**:
  * AUROC: 0.83–0.95 (retinal fluid segmentation)
  * AUROC: 0.94 (progression from intermediate AMD to GA)
  * Sensitivity: 91% (progression from intermediate AMD to GA)
  * Specificity: 80% (progression from intermediate AMD to GA)
  * Accuracy: ~60%–70% (recurrence of nAMD)
  * AUROC: >0.9 (ERM classification)
  * Accuracy: 98% (normal ERM), 84% (stage 3 severity), 81.3% (overall)
  * AUPRC: 0.971 (MacTel Type II detection)
  * AUROC: 0.970 (MacTel Type II detection)
  * Accuracy: 0.898% (MacTel Type II detection)
  * Sensitivity: 0.898 (MacTel Type II detection)
  * Specificity: 0.949 (MacTel Type II detection)

* **ChatGPT-Assisted DR Detection and Severity Classification**:
  * AUPRC: 0.81 (DR severity classification)
  * Precision: 81.81% (DR severity classification)
  * Recall: 72.83% (DR severity classification)
  * Precision: 84.48% (DR detection)
  * Recall: 84.48% (DR detection)
  * AUPRC: 0.90 (DR detection)

* **Other Imaging Modalities**:
  * Accuracy: 0.8208 (NP detection), 0.8338 (NV detection) in UWFA
  * R2: 0.33–0.38 (VA change prediction), 0.37–0.45 (actual VA at 2 years)
  * AUROC: 0.85–0.87 (15-letter visual gain), 0.67–0.79 (15-letter visual loss)
evaluation/comparison:
**Comparison Performed: Yes**

* **Vision Transformers (ViT) vs. Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs)**:
  * ViT models were compared against CNN models for classifying diabetic retinopathy (DR) in both color fundus photographs (CFPs) and optical coherence tomography (OCT) scans.
  * ViT models demonstrated superior performance in certain cases, particularly when pretrained at sufficient scale and transferred to specific tasks with fewer images.

* **Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) vs. Traditional Models**:
  * GANs were used to address imbalanced datasets in OCT images, outperforming traditional models trained on unbalanced datasets.
  * GANs were also used for synthesizing CFPs and OCT scans, improving the performance of models trained on these synthetic datasets.

* **Foundation Models vs. Traditional Supervised Models**:
  * The RETFound foundation model, pretrained with 1.6 million retinal images, was compared to traditional supervised and self-supervised models pretrained on ImageNet datasets.
  * RETFound outperformed these traditional models in detecting referrable DR, age-related macular degeneration (AMD), glaucoma, and predicting systemic diseases.

* **Large Language Models (LLMs) for Text-Related Tasks**:
  * ChatGPT was used for generating computer language scripts and analyzing data to develop AI models for DR detection and severity classification.
  * ChatGPT's performance was compared to traditional coding methods, highlighting its potential for advanced data analysis without extensive coding requirements.

* **Segment Anything Model (SAM)**:
  * SAM, an open-sourced foundation model developed by Meta, was compared to traditional segmentation techniques.
  * SAM aims to perform segmentation on any kind of image without requiring extensive training, demonstrating its generalizability and ease of use.
